reasoning: The base rate of a lethal confrontation between India and China over a two-month period is extremely low (historically less than 1%), and geopolitical conditions make it even less likely now. In October 2024, the two countries reached an agreement leading to military disengagement at key friction points (Depsang and Demchok) along the Line of Actual Control (LAC). Recent reporting from June and July 2025 confirms that the relationship continues to stabilize, with both governments actively engaging in diplomatic dialogues—including reviews of the border situation and preparation for high-level "Special Representative" boundary talks. Frontline troops have been pulled back to buffer zones, significantly reducing the chance of accidental encounters or hand-to-hand skirmishes. In the absence of any recent inflammatory rhetoric or breakdowns in the 2024 pact, the likelihood of a lethal clash before September 30, 2025, is negligibly small. I am submitting 0.05 to reflect this near certainty while strictly complying with the minimum probability bounds rule.
